evals.name: report-and-project
evals.prompt: My OpenClaw costs have been creeping up and I don't really know why. My usage log is at /sessions/serene-dreamy-ritchie/mnt/OpenClaw/skills/token-cockpit/sample_usage.jsonl — can you break down where the money's going by model and tell me roughly what I'm on track to spend this month?
evals.expected_output: Runs token_cockpit.py report on the log. Reports a total spend (~$15) and a monthly projection (~$43), and identifies the most expensive model (claude-opus at roughly half the cost). Notes the projection is an extrapolation.

assertions.text: Identifies claude-opus as the most expensive model
Runs the bundled token_cockpit.py tool rather than estimating by hand

evals.name: find-savings
evals.prompt: I want to cut my token bill. Using the usage log at /sessions/serene-dreamy-ritchie/mnt/OpenClaw/skills/token-cockpit/sample_usage.jsonl, where am I wasting money and how much could I realistically save?
evals.expected_output: Runs token_cockpit.py route (and/or simulate). Identifies that many small calls are running on claude-opus and could move to a cheaper model, quantifies an estimated savings figure, and caveats that savings assume equal tokens and adequate quality.

assertions.text: Identifies small calls on a premium model (claude-opus) as the main waste
Includes a caveat about verifying quality / that savings are estimates
